notifications.title: Interview Scheduled
notifications.message: Your interview with Google is scheduled.

notifications.title: New Job Match
notifications.message: You have a new job match for Frontend Developer.

aptitude.question: A train running at the speed of 60 km/hr crosses a pole in 9 seconds. What is the length of the train?
aptitude.explanation: Speed = (60 * 5/18) m/sec = 50/3 m/sec. Distance = Speed * Time = (50/3 * 9) = 150 m.

aptitude.question: The cost price of 20 articles is the same as the selling price of x articles. If the profit is 25%, then the value of x is:
aptitude.explanation: Let CP of each article be Re. 1 CP of x articles = Rs. x. SP of x articles = Rs. 20. Profit = 20 - x. (20-x)/x * 100 = 25 => x=16

technical.question: Which of the following is not an OOPS concept?
technical.explanation: Exception is a mechanism to handle runtime errors, not an OOP concept.

technical.question: What is a virtual memory?
technical.explanation: Virtual memory is a memory management capability of an OS that uses hardware and software to allow a computer to compensate for physical memory shortages.

coding.question: Write a function to reverse a string in place.
coding.explanation: Using Python slicing `[::-1]` allows reversing the string easily.

coding.question: Find the missing number in a given integer array of 1 to n.
coding.explanation: Calculate the expected sum of numbers from 1 to n using the formula n(n+1)/2. Then subtract the actual sum of the array.

hr.question: Tell me about yourself.
hr.answer: This is a subjective question. Focus on your professional background, key achievements, and why you are a good fit for the role.

hr.question: What are your greatest strengths and weaknesses?
hr.answer: Subjective. Mention a strength relevant to the job. For weakness, mention something you are actively working to improve.

questions.question: How does Google Search work?
questions.answer: Google Search works in three basic stages: Crawling (finding pages), Indexing (analyzing and storing), and Serving (ranking and showing results).

questions.question: What is your favorite Google product and how would you improve it?
questions.answer: Subjective. Requires good product sense, identifying pain points, and proposing realistic solutions.

questions.question: Explain how you would design a system like Microsoft Teams.
questions.answer: Involves discussing microservices architecture, WebSockets for real-time chat, load balancers, database choices (NoSQL for chat history), and handling high concurrency.

chatbotQA.answer: Your resume should ideally be 1 page long. Highlight your skills, projects, and relevant experience. Make sure to include links to your GitHub and LinkedIn profiles.

ATS (Applicant Tracking System) parses your resume for keywords. To get a high score, use standard headings, avoid complex formatting (like columns or tables), and include keywords relevant to the job description.

Aptitude rounds usually test quantitative, logical, and verbal skills. Practice topics like Time & Work, Speed Distance, Profit Loss, and Number Series. We have a dedicated Aptitude section in the Interview Preparation module.
For technical rounds, strong fundamentals in Data Structures and Algorithms (DSA) are crucial. Focus on Arrays, Strings, Linked Lists, Trees, and DP. Also, brush up on OOPs, DBMS, and OS.
When asked to introduce yourself, give a brief overview of your education, technical skills, a major project, and your career goals. Keep it under 2 minutes and be confident.

For strengths, mention traits like adaptability or quick learning with examples. For weaknesses, state a genuine weakness but also mention the steps you are taking to overcome it (e.g., public speaking and joining a toastmasters club).
As a fresher, it's best to say you expect salary as per company norms. Focus more on the role and learning opportunities rather than negotiating salary right away.
Mock interviews simulate the real environment. Use our Mock Tests section to practice. Record yourself, analyze your mistakes, and work on your communication skills.
Build projects that solve real-world problems. Full-stack web applications, machine learning models, or utility tools are great. Make sure you can explain every line of your code.
A strong GitHub profile shows your coding activity. Pin your best repositories. A professional LinkedIn profile helps recruiters find you; make sure to list your skills and ask for recommendations.
On-campus drives have less competition compared to off-campus. For off-campus, leverage referrals, apply via careers pages, and participate in hackathons or coding contests.
Platforms like LeetCode, HackerRank, and Codeforces are excellent for DSA practice. Aim to solve at least 200-300 questions focusing on medium difficulty on LeetCode.
Certifications from reputed organizations (like AWS, Google, or Microsoft) can add value to your resume, but practical knowledge and projects weigh much more in technical interviews.
Most companies have a strict cutoff of 60% or 6.5 CGPA without active backlogs. Keep your CGPA above 7.0 or 7.5 to be eligible for the majority of top companies.
Rejections are a part of the process. Ask for feedback if possible, analyze the areas you struggled with, and prepare those topics before your next interview. Don't lose hope!

For Java interviews, ensure you know the core concepts, common design patterns, and can write syntactically correct code on a whiteboard. Practice Java specific interview questions in our technical section.

For Python interviews, ensure you know the core concepts, common design patterns, and can write syntactically correct code on a whiteboard. Practice Python specific interview questions in our technical section.

For Javascript interviews, ensure you know the core concepts, common design patterns, and can write syntactically correct code on a whiteboard. Practice Javascript specific interview questions in our technical section.

For React interviews, ensure you know the core concepts, common design patterns, and can write syntactically correct code on a whiteboard. Practice React specific interview questions in our technical section.

For Node interviews, ensure you know the core concepts, common design patterns, and can write syntactically correct code on a whiteboard. Practice Node specific interview questions in our technical section.

For Sql interviews, ensure you know the core concepts, common design patterns, and can write syntactically correct code on a whiteboard. Practice Sql specific interview questions in our technical section.

For Mongodb interviews, ensure you know the core concepts, common design patterns, and can write syntactically correct code on a whiteboard. Practice Mongodb specific interview questions in our technical section.

For Aws interviews, ensure you know the core concepts, common design patterns, and can write syntactically correct code on a whiteboard. Practice Aws specific interview questions in our technical section.

For Docker interviews, ensure you know the core concepts, common design patterns, and can write syntactically correct code on a whiteboard. Practice Docker specific interview questions in our technical section.
TCS National Qualifier Test (NQT) focuses heavily on basic programming, aptitude, and verbal ability. Be prepared for a managerial round as well.
Infosys interviews focus on OOPs, DBMS, and puzzles. Their coding round usually has 2-3 medium difficulty questions.
Wipro's selection process includes an online assessment (Aptitude + Coding + Written English) followed by Technical and HR rounds.
Cognizant GenC tests your foundational CS knowledge and logical reasoning. GenC Next focuses more on advanced coding and problem-solving skills.
Amazon focuses on Leadership Principles and high-level DSA (Trees, Graphs, DP). Practice writing clean code and explaining time/space complexities.
Microsoft interviews emphasize algorithms, system design, and debugging. You should be very comfortable with standard data structures.
Google coding interviews are tough and focus on dynamic programming, graphs, and complex algorithms. They also evaluate 'Googliness' (cultural fit).
